item.description: A small, consistent animation vocabulary now runs across the app, so things move in a calm, on-style way. Toasts and banners glide in and out, and switching workspace scope cross-fades instead of snapping. - Toasts and banners animate both in and out - Workspace re-scoping cross-fades smoothly - Honors your system "reduce motion" setting

item.title: Bring your Slack data into xNet
item.description: Moving from Slack just got easier. A new Slack connector imports your channels and message history into native xNet messages, and a Slack-compatibility layer translates Slack formatting so existing integrations are easier to carry across. - Import Slack channels and message history into native xNet channels - Slack mrkdwn and Block Kit messages translate to xNet-flavored markdown - A migration guide with an honest compatibility matrix

item.title: Cleaner page editor typography
item.description: Page documents now read like Notion or Obsidian. Body text, bullets, numbered lists, to-dos, and blockquotes all share one clean left edge, the text column is centered, and the page title has a clear hierarchy. - Bullets, numbered lists, to-dos, and blockquotes align to a single left edge - Centered text column with consistent vertical rhythm between blocks - Checkboxes sit centered on their first line; the page title now clearly outranks H1

item.title: Linear-grade Tasks
item.description: The Tasks surface gets a Linear-style overhaul — faster in-surface navigation, multi-select bulk actions, a detail slide-over, and powerful filtering and grouping. - In-surface sub-nav (All Issues / My Issues / Triage) plus the Projects list - Multi-select with a floating bulk-action bar — set status, priority, or assignee in one batch - Filter bar, Display Options (group / order / density), Space-to-peek, and a detail slide-over - Opt-in violet "Linear" accent theme (Settings → Appearance → Accent)

item.title: See your local agent in the AI panel
item.description: The AI panel now shows the local bridge agent and its connection status, with a picker to choose which agent handles your requests. - Live status for the local bridge agent - Pick which agent runs (your own Claude Code, Codex, …)

item.title: xNet adapts to your phone
item.description: On a small screen, xNet now uses a content-first mobile layout — bottom navigation and slide-in panels — instead of the desktop grid. - Bottom navigation and edge sheets on mobile - A content-first shell that gets out of the way - The desktop experience is unchanged

item.title: Kick off agentic code tasks from xNet
item.description: The local bridge gained a run endpoint that executes a full agentic code task end to end, powering one-click dev tasks from the app. - A single call drives an end-to-end agent task - Foundation for in-app developer workflows

item.title: Paid plugins
item.description: Plugin authors can now sell their plugins — a licensing spine plus Stripe Connect payouts, with a fail-closed install gate that checks entitlements. - DID-bound plugin licenses - Stripe Connect payouts (publishers keep their own account) - Installs are gated on a valid license

item.title: Plugins run on the Labs runtime
item.description: Plugin code now executes on the same sandboxed Labs runtime, unifying how user code runs and receives capabilities across the app. - Shared sandbox and runtime for plugins and Labs - Consistent capability grants for all user code

item.title: Agent-native Connectors
item.description: Connectors sync an external service into governed nodes and expose agent tools — your AI works with policy-checked data while the access token stays in the hub. - Sync external services into governed nodes - Agent tools are surfaced to the AI automatically - Tokens stay server-side; the agent sees only what policy allows

item.title: Review AI edits before they apply
item.description: AI edits in the editor now show a preview with an approve or reject gate, so nothing changes your document until you say so. - Diff preview of the proposed change - Approve or reject before it lands

item.title: AI edits, right inside the editor
item.description: Select text in the page editor and ask AI to rewrite, summarize, or transform it — inline, without leaving the document. - Trigger AI transforms on a selection - Uses your configured AI provider

item.title: A new look — cosmic-web app icons
item.description: xNet has fresh cosmic-web app icons and a new favicon across the desktop app and the website. - New app icons on desktop - New favicon and marks on the site

item.title: An agentic dev loop in your terminal
item.description: The new xnet code command runs an isolated edit → validate → commit loop with your own agent, so you can vibe-code changes to xNet from the CLI. - Each task runs in an isolated git worktree - Runs the validation gate, commits on pass and rolls back on fail

item.title: Your agent can use your workspace
item.description: When you drive a local agent from xNet, it now gets governed tools to read and write your workspace — with writes gated by policy and your token staying local. - Workspace read/write tools exposed over MCP - Writes pass through a policy guardrail

item.title: A richer, interactive changelog
item.description: The changelog page now shows the actual UI that shipped — screenshots pulled straight from CI — and you can filter, search, and click through them. - Auto-pulled screenshots, before/after sliders, and a click-to-zoom lightbox - Filter by tag and search across every entry - JSON and RSS feeds for the full history

item.title: Your Labs become AI tools
item.description: Tools you build in Labs are now available to the assistant, so your own automations can be called directly from AI chat. - Lab agent-tools surface to the AI automatically - One ladder from a Lab to an AI tool

item.title: AI that can act on your workspace
item.description: The assistant gained a tool surface, @-mention providers, and recommendations — so it can reference your data and take actions, not just chat. - Plugin and Lab capabilities exposed to the AI as tools - Extensible @-mentions in chat - Contextual recommendations

item.title: Drive your own coding agent from xNet
item.description: A new local bridge lets xNet drive your own Claude Code or Codex CLI — bring your own agent, no extra API key, and your token never leaves your machine. - Runs your local Claude Code / Codex from xNet's UI - Loopback-only, OpenAI-compatible streaming - No model cost to xNet — it uses your own agent

item.title: One trust model across plugins and Labs
item.description: Plugins and Labs now share a single trust and provenance system, so “is this code trusted?” is answered the same way everywhere in xNet. - Unified provenance and trust checks for plugins and Labs - Consistent install and run consent across the app

item.title: Automated changelog & in-app "What’s New"
item.description: xNet now keeps a changelog you can actually read. Every release is summarized here, on the website, and inside the app — so you always know what changed when a new version lands. - A public changelog page with a JSON and RSS feed you can subscribe to - An in-app "What’s New" panel that flags updates since your last visit - Release notes on the desktop app are now written in plain language, not raw commit logs - UI screenshots captured by CI can be embedded directly in changelog entries

item.title: AI chat that knows your workspace
item.description: The in-app assistant now reads your own workspace — your notes, tasks, contacts, and more — so its answers are grounded in your data, with citations. It also remembers the conversation instead of treating every message as the first. - Answers are grounded in your workspace and cite the items they draw from - Conversations have memory — the assistant sees the whole thread, not just your last message - A clear "reads workspace" badge and privacy note explain what it can see

item.title: Reliability you can see for managed hubs
item.description: xNet Cloud now measures uptime against real service-level objectives, ties upgrades to error budgets, and runs backup restore drills — the operational backbone behind the managed fleet. - Measured SLIs/SLOs for each managed hub - Error-budget-gated rollouts - Routine backup restore drills

item.title: Plugin ecosystem: scaffolding, marketplace & trust
item.description: Building and sharing plugins is now first-class. A scaffolder gets you from zero to a working plugin in seconds, and an enforceable capability model keeps installs safe. - xnet plugin scaffold command and an AI "script → plugin" path - Capability enforcement, semver compatibility gates and inter-plugin dependencies - A searchable marketplace index with install-consent prompts

item.title: On-device dictation
item.description: Talk to xNet. A new dictation engine turns speech into text locally, with hold-to-talk and private-by-default transcripts. - Zero-dependency dictation engine with hold-to-talk - Private-by-default transcription with full-text search - Pluggable speech backends (bring-your-own engine)

item.title: Extensibility fabric: one home for plugins, labs & AI
item.description: Plugins, labs, the AI surface, and the editor are converging into a single extensibility fabric, so a capability built in one place can be reused everywhere. - Shared trust and consent model across plugins and labs - Plugin contributions and lab tools can be exposed to the AI assistant - Foundations for an AI → Lab → Plugin assembly line

item.title: Faster, cheaper CI
item.description: Behind the scenes, the build pipeline got faster and more reliable — which means features reach you sooner. - Parallelized lint, typecheck and test jobs - Cached browser and native build dependencies

item.title: xNet Cloud — managed hub hosting
item.description: Don’t want to run your own hub? xNet Cloud hosts one for you. A new onboarding flow takes you from signup to a provisioned hub, then lets you claim it from the app. - Marketing and pricing pages for managed hub hosting - Signup → provision → claim flow - Connect the app to your hosted hub from Settings

item.title: A safer foundation for plugins
item.description: Plugins now run on an enforceable platform layer — capability gates, provenance and trust checks, dependency resolution, and a searchable marketplace index — so installing a plugin is governed end to end. - Capability enforcement wraps each plugin's data access - Install-time consent prompts when a plugin needs new capabilities - Inter-plugin dependencies resolve with cycle detection

item.title: xNet AI chat panel works out of the box
item.description: The in-app AI assistant is wired up end-to-end — the chat panel now connects, picks a sensible model tier automatically, and reports errors honestly. - Fixed the connection so the assistant responds on a fresh install - Automatic model-tier selection with a clear status badge - Tier preference persists between sessions

item.title: Cohesive, feature-complete domain apps
item.description: CRM, accounting, tasks, experiments and spaces are now fully editable and consistent. Open any record to see every field, with bespoke views where they help. - Full CRM: contacts, deals, organizations, forecast lanes, quotes and de-dup merge - Finance: account and transaction inspectors, reports tab and budgeting - Tasks: project detail headers and milestone management - A shared inspector substrate (NodePeek + SchemaForm) across every domain

item.title: Plug-and-play billing (Stripe & Bitcoin)
item.description: A provider-agnostic billing layer lets xNet apps take payments via Stripe or Bitcoin (BTCPay) without locking into either. - Stripe and BTCPay adapters behind one payment interface - Signed webhooks with idempotent processing - A useBilling() hook for checkout and subscription status

item.title: Native CRM & ERP foundation
item.description: xNet grew a native CRM — a universal party model, pipelines, deals and activities that reuse the same social and schema primitives as the rest of the app. - Organizations, contacts, pipelines, deals, products and quotes - Append-only activity timeline and contact de-duplication - vCard import/export and GDPR helpers

item.title: Double-entry accounting
item.description: Track your money in xNet. A local-first, double-entry ledger brings accounts, transactions, budgets and reconciliation — with CSV/OFX/QIF import. - Balanced double-entry transactions with integer-precise money - Budgets, reconciliation and chart-of-accounts reports - Import from CSV, OFX and QIF

item.title: Screenshots of every UI change, automatically
item.description: Every pull request that touches the interface now gets before/after screenshots and GIFs captured by CI — the same visuals that can feed this changelog. - Automatic screenshot, diff and GIF capture for changed UI - A sticky gallery comment on each pull request - Durable galleries that survive after merge

item.title: Experiment journal & habit tracker
item.description: Run personal experiments and track habits in xNet, with streaks, correlations and a verdict engine that stays honest about what the data can and can’t show. - Metrics, observations and experiments as first-class data - Streak heatmaps and correlation widgets - A "Today" panel for quick logging
